name: Olivia Chen
goals: Efficiently manage marketing campaigns, keep teams aligned, and meet deadlines consistently.
frustrations: Spreadsheets, missed deadlines, lack of visibility across team tasks, and confusing email threads.
context: Olivia manages multiple marketing projects simultaneously, working with internal designers, copywriters, and external clients. She needs a central hub to track progress and ensure everyone is on the same page.

name: David Lee
goals: Stay organized with client projects, track billable hours, and collaborate effectively with clients.
frustrations: Juggling multiple tools, difficulty tracking time accurately, and communication breakdowns with clients leading to scope creep.
context: David juggles 3-5 clients at any given time, working remotely. He needs a task management system that's flexible, easy to use, and helps him manage his time effectively.

name: Sarah Johnson
goals: Organize coursework, manage extracurricular activities, and collaborate with group members on assignments.
frustrations: Feeling overwhelmed by the volume of assignments, difficulty coordinating group projects, and losing track of deadlines.
context: Sarah is a full-time student taking a heavy course load. She needs a tool to help her prioritize tasks, manage deadlines, and communicate with classmates.

name: George Miller
goals: Keep track of gardening tasks, manage household chores, and share tasks with his wife.
frustrations: Complexity of technology, difficulty remembering passwords, and resistance to learning new digital tools.
context: George is retired and enjoys gardening and maintaining his home. He's looking for a simple way to organize his activities but isn't comfortable with complicated software.

name: Chris Evans
goals: Quickly evaluate the application's features, identify potential bugs, and determine if it's superior to existing solutions.
frustrations: Inefficient workflows, unnecessary features, poor user interface design, and marketing hype that doesn't match reality.
context: Chris is a highly skilled software engineer who is skeptical of new tools. He's quick to identify flaws and demands a high level of performance and usability.
